label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's find out what made Florence Nightingale decide to become a nurse. The text says that "As a child she felt that visiting sick people was both a duty and a pleasure. She enjoyed helping them." So, from a young age, she had a desire to help the sick. Later on, it says "At last mind was made up. 'I'm going to be a nurse,' she decided." It doesn't mention her father's support here. In fact, her father said, "Nursing isn't the right work for a lady," which suggests her father didn't support this choice. Her education in Germany and France is mentioned as something she did after she decided to be a nurse: "And she went to learn nursing in Germany and France." So the education was a result of her decision, not the reason
